data.title: Arbitrum DAO has launched a vote to release 30,766 ETH for the aftermath of the Kelp attack.
data.description: Arbitrum DAO has launched a governance vote to release 30,766 ETH previously frozen to support DeFi United, a post-Kelp DAO recovery plan. These assets, valued at approximately $71.1 million, were frozen by the Arbitrum Security Committee on April 20th and were originally funds transferred into the Arbitrum network by the attackers. If the proposal passes, this will be the largest single investment in the DeFi United initiative. Initially, 16.9 million ARBs have supported the proposal, and there are currently no opposing votes. Voting will continue until May 7th.

data.title: With the US crypto market structure bill nearing completion, ethical controversies are becoming a key issue.
data.description: The legislative process for the US cryptocurrency market structure is accelerating, with Congress pushing for a markup phase as early as mid-May. However, ethical concerns and the cryptocurrency interests associated with Donald Trump remain uncertain about the path forward. Senator Thom Tillis, one of the main proponents, stated that he has asked Senate Banking Committee Chairman Tim Scott to schedule hearings and advance the revision and voting process, while also hoping to release a new version of the bill before deliberation. However, Tillis emphasized that he will oppose the bill's passage if it does not include explicit ethical provisions. With the midterm elections approaching, ethical issues are expected to become a key focus for the Democratic Party.

data.title: Trump: I don't care whether Powell stays on the board.
data.description: US President Trump stated that he wouldn't care if Jerome Powell remained on the Federal Reserve Board of Governors after his term as chairman ended on May 15th, but his term as governor runs until 2028. He indicated his intention to "continue serving as governor for an indefinite period." Meanwhile, Kevin Warsh is expected to receive full Senate approval before Powell's term ends, and Powell's continued tenure could complicate Trump's efforts to reshape the Fed. When asked if he planned to take any action regarding Powell's decision to remain on the board, Trump replied, "No, I don't care if he stays. I just want to make sure Kevin gets the job."

data.title: AI TRENDS | Meta CEO Zuckerberg Details Aggressive AI Plans Amid Market Concerns
data.description:
Meta Platforms CEO Mark Zuckerberg provided new insights into the company's ambitious AI strategy during a company-wide meeting on Thursday, according to Jin10, citing The Wall Street Journal. He addressed the market's negative reaction to Meta's first-quarter performance, attributing the 8% drop in Meta's stock price to investor concerns over increased capital expenditure forecasts and the company's warning of slower growth in the second quarter.

Zuckerberg noted a shift in Meta's advertising business trajectory following the U.S. conflict with Iran in late February. He explained, "If oil prices rise, consumers tend to spend more on oil and gasoline, reducing expenditure on non-essential items, which are typically the focus of advertising." He linked next month's planned layoffs to the need for increased investment in data centers and other AI infrastructure.

Zuckerberg stated, "The company essentially has two cost centers: computing and infrastructure, and personnel. If we allocate more funds to one area to serve our community, it means less capital is available for the other. Therefore, we need to moderately reduce the company's size."

data.title: Data shows that USDT0 users primarily use small amounts, with over 99% holding less than $1,000.
data.description: Latest data from the full-chain stablecoin USDT0 shows that its user base is predominantly retail investors: approximately 99.2% of holders have wallet balances below $1,000, only about 1,200 addresses hold between $100,000 and $1 million, and only 35 addresses hold over $10 million. The report indicates that USDT0's primary use case is small-value cross-chain transfers, with active users primarily engaged in daily transactions rather than large-scale fund flows. However, in terms of transaction volume, transactions exceeding $1 million still account for approximately 68.8% of the total transaction volume. USDT0 was launched by Everdawn Labs and is supported by LayerZero and Tether, pegged 1:1 to USDT. It has now expanded to 23 chains, with a total transaction volume of $86.7 billion, making it the third-largest USDT holder after Binance and OKX. (The Block)

data.title: Bombardier CEO Highlights Company's Financial Turnaround
data.description: Bombardier's CEO, Eric Martel, who took the helm in 2020, has overseen significant changes in the company as it navigated financial challenges. Bloomberg posted on X that Martel now speaks confidently about Bombardier's reduced debt load, marking a notable turnaround from its previous struggles to stave off bankruptcy. The company has undergone substantial restructuring efforts to stabilize its financial position. Martel's leadership has been pivotal in steering Bombardier through these turbulent times, focusing on strategic initiatives to ensure long-term sustainability.

data.title: Arbitrum DAO Votes on Release of Frozen ETH After Kelp DAO Attack
data.description: Arbitrum DAO has initiated a voting process to decide on the release of 30,766 ETH, which was frozen by the Arbitrum Security Council following the Kelp DAO attack. According to NS3.AI, the voting will continue until May 7. In the first hour, the proposal received support from 16.9 million ARB tokens, with no opposition votes recorded. The frozen funds are part of approximately $71.1 million in ETH that the attacker transferred to an Arbitrum One address on April 20.

data.title: U.S. President Trump Directs Shift to Fixed-Price Government Contracts
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has mandated federal agencies to prioritize fixed-price agreements for government procurement. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting Trump's decision to move away from cost-reimbursement contracts, which he argues can result in excessive spending. This directive aims to enhance fiscal responsibility and control over government expenditures.

data.title: Meta Platforms CEO Zuckerberg Announces Team Reductions Amid Sales Impact from Iran Conflict
data.description: Meta Platforms CEO Mark Zuckerberg has announced plans to reduce team sizes across various departments within the company. According to Jin10, Zuckerberg attributed the decision to changes in sales trajectories caused by the ongoing conflict in Iran. The company is adjusting its strategy in response to the evolving geopolitical situation, which has affected its business operations.

data.title: Bitcoin 2026: Focus Shifts from Price to Community Identity
data.description: Bitcoin 2026 has been characterized as an event that emphasizes the identity and community of Bitcoin supporters rather than market-driven factors. According to NS3.AI, the annual gathering resembled a large-scale self-help session more than a traditional market rally, highlighting the evolving focus of the Bitcoin community.

data.title: Air Canada Suspends 2026 Guidance Amid Jet Fuel Price Uncertainty
data.description: Air Canada has announced the suspension of its full-year 2026 financial guidance, citing uncertainties surrounding jet fuel prices. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the airline's decision as a response to fluctuating fuel costs, which have created challenges in forecasting future financial performance. The airline industry has been grappling with volatile fuel prices, impacting operational costs and financial projections. Air Canada's move reflects a cautious approach in navigating these uncertainties, as the company aims to adapt to the evolving economic landscape. The suspension of guidance underscores the broader challenges faced by airlines globally in managing cost pressures and maintaining profitability amid unpredictable market conditions.

data.title: Amazon Considers Reboot of 'The Apprentice' with Trump’s Son as Host
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has acknowledged that Amazon is contemplating a revival of his popular reality television series, 'The Apprentice.' Bloomberg posted on X that the reboot may feature Trump's eldest son in the role of host. The original show, which aired from 2004 to 2017, was known for its competitive format where contestants vied for a job within Trump's organization. The potential reboot reflects ongoing interest in the franchise, although details regarding its production and airing remain undisclosed.

data.title: U.S. President Trump Invites Iraq's Prime Minister Nominee to Washington
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has extended an invitation to Iraq's prime minister nominee to visit Washington following the formation of a new government. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the U.S. administration's efforts to curb Iran's influence in Iraq. This diplomatic move underscores the strategic importance of Iraq in the region and the ongoing geopolitical dynamics involving the U.S. and Iran. The invitation is part of broader U.S. efforts to strengthen ties with Iraq and ensure stability in the Middle East.

data.title: STOCKS | Western Digital Forecasts Higher Quarterly Revenue Amid AI Demand
data.description: Western Digital (WDC.O) has projected that its quarterly revenue will surpass Wall Street expectations, driven by strong demand for data storage from artificial intelligence companies. According to Jin10, the company's stock fell nearly 5% in after-hours trading on Thursday. As of Thursday's close, Western Digital's stock price has more than doubled this year, fueled by significant investments from tech giants in AI infrastructure, which has created substantial demand for its high-capacity hard drives. The company anticipates fourth-quarter revenue to be $3.65 billion, with a variance of $100 million, compared to analysts' average forecast of $3.46 billion. In the third quarter, Western Digital's revenue increased by 45% to $3.34 billion, exceeding the expected $3.25 billion. Adjusted earnings per share were $2.72, surpassing the market expectation of $2.39.

data.title: STOCKS | U.S. Stocks Achieve Best Month in Six Years Amid Global Challenges
data.description: U.S. stocks have successfully navigated a divided Federal Reserve, ongoing conflict in the Middle East, and a series of significant tech earnings to conclude their most successful month in six years. Wall Street Journal (Markets) posted on X, highlighting the resilience of the stock market despite these challenges. The Federal Reserve's internal divisions over monetary policy have created uncertainty, yet the market has shown strength. Meanwhile, geopolitical tensions in the Middle East continue to pose risks, but have not deterred investor confidence. Additionally, the tech sector's earnings reports have played a crucial role in bolstering market performance, with several major companies exceeding expectations. This combination of factors has contributed to a remarkable month for U.S. stocks, demonstrating their ability to withstand global pressures and economic uncertainties.

data.title: U.S. President Donald Trump Considers Candidates for Attorney General Role
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump is known for transforming his personnel decisions into high-stakes competitions, akin to reality TV shows. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that the selection process for the next attorney general is following a similar pattern. The decision is anticipated to be a strategic move, reflecting Trump's approach to governance and his penchant for dramatic flair in political appointments.

data.title: STOCKS | Apple Reports Higher EPS for Q2 2026
data.description: Apple Inc. (AAPL.O) announced its earnings per share (EPS) for the second quarter of the 2026 fiscal year, revealing a figure of $2.01. According to Jin10, this marks an increase from the $1.65 EPS recorded during the same period last year. The market had anticipated an EPS of $1.94 for this quarter.

data.title: U.S. President Trump Considers Reducing Troops in NATO Countries
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has suggested the possibility of reducing American military personnel stationed in other NATO member countries. Bloomberg posted on X that this statement follows his recent announcement of a review of U.S. troop levels in Germany. The potential drawdown reflects ongoing discussions about the distribution of U.S. military forces in Europe and their strategic deployment within NATO. This move could have significant implications for U.S. relations with its NATO allies and the overall security dynamics in the region.

data.title: Solana's Carrot Protocol to Shut Down Following Drift Hack Impact
data.description: Carrot, a decentralized finance (DeFi) protocol on the Solana blockchain, has announced its decision to cease operations. According to NS3.AI, the protocol cited the aftermath of the Drift hack as the primary reason for its closure, stating that continued operation is no longer feasible. Users have been advised to withdraw any remaining funds by May 14.

data.title: Bitcoin Rises to $76,420 Amid Market Cap Increase
data.description: The cryptocurrency market capitalization increased by 1.1% to reach $2.63 trillion on Thursday, with Bitcoin trading at $76,420 following a three-day decline. According to NS3.AI, data from SoSoValue indicated that U.S. spot Bitcoin ETFs experienced net outflows of $138 million on April 29. Meanwhile, the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) maintained interest rates between 3.50% and 3.75% in an 8-4 vote.
